title: The Parachute Regiment
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A ragtag company of British paratroopers drops behind enemy lines on D-Day only to discover the intelligence was wrong.
long_description:
On the night of June 5, 1944, a company of British paratroopers from the 6th Airborne Division drops into Normandy as the vanguard of the D-Day invasion. Their mission: seize a vital bridge and hold it until the commandos arrive. But the intelligence is catastrophically wrong. The bridge is heavily fortified, a Panzer division is billeted in the surrounding countryside, and the company is scattered across the wrong drop zone.

Sergeant Tom Miller, a battle-hardened veteran carrying the weight of too many dead men; Private Leo Flynn, a nineteen-year-old replacement from Bethnal Green who has never seen combat; and Lieutenant James Ashford, a newly commissioned officer whose book-learned tactics are tested against the brutal reality of war. Cut off, outnumbered, and running out of time, they must hold together their shattered unit, navigate enemy territory, and find a way to accomplish the mission or die trying.

The Parachute Regiment is a story about men under pressure, the bonds forged in extreme circumstances, and the cost of doing what must be done when every option has failed. It is a war drama told at ground level, through the eyes of the men who fought and the ones who did not come home.
